p: Capmonster.cloud module for Node.js — at least 2x cheaper, up to 30x faster than manual recognition services.

c: bun add node-capmonster
npm install node-capmonster
yarn add node-capmonster
pnpm add node-capmonster

p: Every captcha type follows the same pattern:
Create a client with your API key
All task classes inherit from CapmonsterClient, which provides the getBalance method:

c:
const balance = await client.getBalance()
console.log(`Balance: $${balance}`)

p: If a solution was incorrect, you can report it:
Some captcha types require a valid browser User-Agent. You can fetch the current one from CapMonster Cloud:

c: const userAgent = await client.getUserAgent()

p: Set the maximum time (in seconds) to wait for a solution:

c: client.setTimeout(60) // 60 seconds (must be between 1 and 300)

p: Choose a captcha type from the Usage section to see detailed examples.
Detailed guides for each supported captcha type.
Select a captcha type from the sidebar to get started.
Solve Google reCAPTCHA v2 challenges. Tokens are valid for 60 seconds after solving.
websiteKey: Look for data-sitekey in the HTML, or the k= parameter in the reCAPTCHA iframe URL.
isInvisible: Check for size=invisible in the captcha element.
Solve Google reCAPTCHA v2 Enterprise challenges with additional enterprise-specific parameters.
Solve Google reCAPTCHA v3 challenges. Always uses built-in proxies (no custom proxy support).
websiteKey: Found in api.js?render=THIS_ONE in the page source.
Do NOT load the captcha iframe to extract the blob value — this will invalidate it. Extract the blob from network requests instead.
Arkose Labs subdomain (domain only, no https://)
Solve GeeTest v3 and v4 captcha challenges.
For GeeTest v3, the challenge value must be fresh for each task. Using an expired challenge will result in ERROR_TOKEN_EXPIRED and you will still be charged.
Solve Cloudflare Turnstile captchas and Cloudflare Challenge pages ("Just a moment...").
For Cloudflare Challenge pages that return a token:
Returns a cf_clearance cookie. Your own proxy is required.
token or cf_clearance for Challenge pages
Solve image-based text captchas via OCR.

amazon, apple, botdetect, facebook, gmx, google, hotmail, mailru, okeng, okrus, partiallyblur, ramblerrus, ramblerrusnew, solvemedia, steam, vk, whatsapp, wikimedia_paddle_ensemble, yandex, yandexnew, yandexwave, captcha_math, sat, universal
Solve Amazon AWS WAF captcha challenges. Supports both standard and challenge modes.
true to get solution as cookies
Solve DataDome captcha challenges. Proxy is required.
Cookies to inject, keyed by domain
Solve Imperva/Incapsula anti-bot challenges. Proxy is required.
Cookies to inject, keyed by domain
Solve complex image-based captchas such as reCAPTCHA image grids and FunCaptcha images.
You must provide at least one of imageUrls or imagesBase64, and at least one of metadata.Task or metadata.TaskDefinition.
Array of booleans — true = click that image position
Configure proxy and User-Agent for tasks that support them.
MTCaptcha, Prosopo, Yidun, Altcha, Basilisk, Castle, Hunt, RecaptchaClick, TurnstileWaitroom, TSPD
Pass proxy directly in the task configuration. This allows using different proxies for different tasks with the same client instance.
If you omit the proxy field, CapMonster Cloud uses built-in proxies:
If the proxy is authorized by IP, whitelist 65.21.190.34.
You can import the ProxyConfig type for type-safe proxy configuration:
setGlobalProxy and unsetProxy are deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Use per-task proxy instead.
The global proxy applies to all tasks created by that client instance. It is still functional but will emit a deprecation warning.
When both per-task and global proxy are set, per-task proxy takes precedence:
Per-task proxy — if proxy is provided in the task config
Global proxy — if setGlobalProxy was called and no per-task proxy
Proxyless — if neither is set (uses CapMonster Cloud built-in proxies)

c: client.setUserAgent("Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) ...")
client.unsetUserAgent()

p: Fetch the current valid Windows UA from CapMonster Cloud:
User-Agent settings persist until explicitly unset with unsetUserAgent(). They apply to all tasks created by that client instance.

c:
const ua = await client.getUserAgent()
client.setUserAgent(ua)

p: Instead of polling for results, you can receive a POST callback when a task completes.
The callback payload is identical to the getTaskResult response. Your server must accept the request and respond within 2 seconds.
The callback URL applies to all tasks created after it is set. Call unsetCallbackUrl() to stop receiving callbacks.

c: client.unsetCallbackUrl()
